title: API Resources, Object Identity, Schemas, Desired State, and Status · Control-Plane Trace: K8s Schema Defaulting Validation
summary: A shared cluster rejects and defaults manifests differently across supported API versions while stale status leads a release team toward the wrong repair. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s schema defaulting validation increment in versioned API object contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict decoding, defaulting, validation, admission, conversion, persistence, and unknown-field behavior from the published schema.
Use namespace, name, UID, resourceVersion, generation, timestamps, and managed fields to distinguish identity, concurrency, and history.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern

A defensible solution applies K8s Schema Defaulting Validation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned API object contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-schema-defaulting-validation — Predict decoding, defaulting, validation, admission, conversion, persistence, and unknown-field behavior from the published schema.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-api-objects-schema-status-2
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-api-objects-schema-status-2
    artifact: api-object.yaml
    decision: worked k8s api objects schema status 2
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: make an admission dependency unavailable during an upgrade
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      replicas: 3
      status-evidence: observedGeneration-and-conditions
    verify: kubectl explain deployment.spec --api-version=apps/v1 && kubectl get deployment status-api -o json
    failure: Reject valid-YAML-only evidence, stale status, unknown fields, or an API selected without feature-state and deprecation review.
    repair: Use live discovery and schema, separate spec from status, compare generation with observedGeneration, and preserve object UID and resourceVersion.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Valid YAML syntax guarantees that the API server accepts and stores every supplied field. This fails the K8s Schema Defaulting Validation contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-schema-defaulting-validation-6onir5
  - competency: k8s-schema-defaulting-validation
    artifact: api-object.yaml
    decision: schema defaulting validation
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: remove DNS egress from an otherwise ready workload
    retained-proof: object UID generation observedGeneration and status conditions
    case-key: 6onir5
    change: |
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      replicas: 3
      status-evidence: observedGeneration-and-conditions
    verify: kubectl explain deployment.spec --api-version=apps/v1 && kubectl get deployment status-api -o json
    failure: Reject valid-YAML-only evidence, stale status, unknown fields, or an API selected without feature-state and deprecation review.
    repair: Use live discovery and schema, separate spec from status, compare generation with observedGeneration, and preserve object UID and resourceVersion.
